summary: Decompiled a substantial portion of the in-game magnify/offscreen indicator system in src/melee/if/ifmagnify.c, replacing placeholder declarations for ifMagnify_802FB73C, ifMagnify_802FB8C0, ifMagnify_802FBBDC, and ifMagnify_802FC3C0 with typed C implementations, and fully matching ifMagnify_802FC870. The PR also refined ifMagnify-related types by introducing ifMagnifyPlayer, correcting the model descriptor field, and modeling per-player image descriptors and bitfield state. decomp.dev reported one new full match and seven additional improvements, with the largest gains in ifMagnify_802FBBDC, ifMagnify_802FB8C0, ifMagnify_802FC3C0, and ifMagnify_802FB73C.
what_changed: Implemented ifMagnify_802FB73C with Vec2 inputs/outputs to clamp an offscreen direction to a screen-edge position and set player->state.unk to edge/category values.

Implemented ifMagnify_802FB8C0 as the GX callback for magnify arrow display, using Player_GetEntity, ftLib_80086A58 screen coordinates, atan2f rotation, HSD_JObjSetTranslate, player/team color lookup, and un_802FD928/un_802FD9D8 arrow coloring calls.

Implemented ifMagnify_802FBBDC to render magnified offscreen fighter images using an orthographic camera, stage camera bounds, fighter world/interest positions, background color interpolation from Ground_801C06xx color providers, ftDrawCommon_80080C28 draw passes, lb_800122C8 image copy/update, and per-player offscreen state tracking.

Implemented ifMagnify_802FC3C0 to create and initialize per-player magnify GObjs, load the model joint from ifMagnify_804A1DE0.model_desc->joint, set a GX link to ifMagnify_802FB8C0, locate child/player JObjs, allocate/copy per-slot HSD_ImageDesc buffers, and tint materials using player/team color.
Updated ifMagnify_802FC870 to load the "lupe" archive section through ifAll_802F3690 and initialize six player magnify objects in a do-while loop, producing a full match according to the bot report.
Added static data for JObj assert strings, multiple float/double extern constants, a Ground color callback table, and the packed color ID lookup table ifMagnify_803F984C.
Changed ifmagnify.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures and added dolphin/mtx.h for Vec2.

Refactored src/melee/if/types.h by adding typedef struct ifMagnifyPlayer and changing struct ifMagnify to contain DynamicModelDesc* model_desc, ifMagnifyPlayer player[6], image_descs[5 * 0x18], and final padding.
smart_moves: Separated per-player magnify state into a named ifMagnifyPlayer typedef, making pointer arithmetic like player - ifMagnify_804A1DE0.player and slot-indexed state easier to express cleanly.

Modeled the state byte as bitfields for is_offscreen, ignore_offscreen, and a six-bit unk field, matching the way the implementation tests and updates offscreen state without inventing stronger semantic names.
Preserved evidence-based placeholder naming for unclear fields and constants, such as state.unk and ifMagnify_804DDBxx, while still typing surrounding behavior enough to improve matching.

Used known engine helpers rather than open-coded behavior: HSD_CObjGetOrtho/SetOrtho/SetCurrent, HSD_JObjSetRotationZ/SetTranslate, ftLib screen/world helpers, Player team/slot functions, and Ground color providers.

Copied player 0's HSD_ImageDesc for later slots and allocated aligned texture buffers using GXGetTexBufferSize and HSD_MemAlloc, reflecting the runtime layout instead of treating the image descriptor storage as opaque padding.

Retained decompiler-sensitive control flow in ifMagnify_802FC870 by changing the initialization loop to a do-while and introducing a local HSD_Archive** archive, which helped the function reach 100% match.
decomp_lessons: For HUD/offscreen indicator code, matching may require typing cross-system calls across IF, camera, stage bounds, fighter draw, player/team color, HSD CObj/JObj/DObj/MObj, archive loading, and texture image descriptors.
When a global struct is accessed as repeated slot-sized records, extracting a named per-slot struct can clarify both pointer arithmetic and header declarations while preserving exact offsets.
Do not prematurely over-name unknown state categories: this PR kept state.unk where the values were clearly used for edge/arrow behavior but not semantically proven.
Archive section names may need to be literal strings in matched code; this diff replaced a prior ifMagnify_804D57E8 reference with "lupe" in lbArchive_LoadSections.

Camera-bound/offscreen rendering code often depends on repeated stage-bound helper calls and float classification/blending logic; preserving that structure can be more important than simplifying the math.
Per-player texture/image storage can be represented inside the owning global struct once size evidence is available; here five copied HSD_ImageDesc-sized records account for nonzero slots.
naming_conventions: Newly typed functions retain address-based project names: ifMagnify_802FB73C, ifMagnify_802FB8C0, ifMagnify_802FBBDC, ifMagnify_802FC3C0, ifMagnify_802FC870.
The PR uses the module prefix ifMagnify for globals, structs, and functions.
Unresolved data symbols remain address-based, such as ifMagnify_804DDB08, ifMagnify_804DDB28, ifMagnify_804DDB50, and ifMagnify_803F984C.
The new per-player type follows the module-type style ifMagnifyPlayer rather than leaving it as an anonymous nested struct.

Fields with supported semantics received descriptive names such as gobj, jobj, idesc, model_desc, image_descs, is_offscreen, and ignore_offscreen; uncertain fields remain x4/x8/xC/x10, pad, and state.unk.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed ifMagnify_802FC870 from a for loop to an initialized i plus do-while loop, matching the expected control flow and yielding 100.00% for that function.
Introduced an HSD_Archive** local before lbArchive_LoadSections, which likely helped reproduce register/stack behavior for ifMagnify_802FC870.
Used explicit temporary float variables in ifMagnify_802FB73C and ifMagnify_802FBBDC rather than algebraically simplifying, preserving compiler-like evaluation order.
Kept many float constants as extern sdata2 symbols instead of literals where the original binary appears to load them from data.
Used a packed s32 lookup table cast to u8* for ifMagnify_803F984C color IDs, matching the apparent data layout of four byte-sized color indices per table entry.
Added static assert-string data for HSD_JObj quaternion checks: "!(jobj->flags & JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION)", "jobj.h", and "jobj".
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code remained 66.41% overall with +120 bytes, one new match, and seven improvements.
Bot-reported new full match: main/melee/if/ifmagnify ifMagnify_802FC870 improved from 94.33% to 100.00%.

Bot-reported major improvements: ifMagnify_802FBBDC 0.00% to 85.63%, ifMagnify_802FB8C0 0.00% to 94.04%, ifMagnify_802FC3C0 0.00% to 91.33%, ifMagnify_802FB73C 0.00% to 98.87%, .data 0.00% to 56.85%, .sdata2 15.38% to 55.00%, and .sdata 0.00% to 70.59%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching ifMagnify_802FBBDC, ifMagnify_802FB8C0, ifMagnify_802FC3C0, or ifMagnify_802FB73C.
Search for follow-up renames of ifMagnifyPlayer.state.unk to determine the semantic meaning of values 1 through 4.
Look for PRs that replace ifMagnify_804DDBxx constants with named constants or documented screen-edge bounds.
Check whether ifMagnify_803F984C color interpolation table receives a named type or enum for Ground color indices.
Search for additional archive-loading matches that required literal section strings like "lupe" and do-while initialization loops.
